title: Restore GitHub Actions runner start capability
goal: Resolve the GitHub account payment or spending-limit condition that prevents Actions jobs from starting, then prove that an unchanged representative workflow obtains a runner and executes real steps.

approval.note: Account billing or spending-limit changes require direct operator action. Repository workflow changes are not an acceptable substitute unless independently justified.

acceptance.assertion: The account-level payment, budget or spending-limit cause is identified from GitHub billing and Actions evidence without misclassifying it as a code or test failure.
Only the necessary billing or spending-limit setting is corrected by the operator; no repository is made public/private and no workflow is weakened merely to bypass the block.
A representative unchanged workflow starts a runner, records at least one executed step and produces logs.
The workflow result is reported separately from runner-start capability; a genuine test failure remains a test failure.
Previously locally validated but runner-blocked Infra PR evidence is reclassified with the new runner proof; no historical result is rewritten.

metadata.observed_annotation: The job was not started because recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased.
